title: The Marvel of Photosynthesis: Nature's Solar Power
summary: Photosynthesis is a process by which plants, algae, and some bacteria convert light energy into chemical energy to fuel their activities. Essential for life on Earth as it produces oxygen and organic compounds.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: Photosynthesis is a biochemical process in which plants, algae, and certain bacteria convert carbon dioxide and water into organic compounds using sunlight.
key_points: It's the method used by green plants to produce food

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body: In chloroplasts of plant cells, light is absorbed by chlorophyll pigments during photosynthesis. Energy converts water and carbon dioxide into glucose through the Calvin cycle.
key_points: Occurs in two stages - Light-dependent reactions & Calvin Cycle

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: Photosynthesis is vital for life on Earth as not only does it provide oxygen, but also forms the foundation of food chains and stores energy from sunlight in chemical bonds.
key_points: All aerobic organisms rely indirectly or directly on photosynthetic activity

key_facts: Photosynthesis takes place primarily in the leaves of plants, specifically within their chloroplast cells.
